articles.title: VC RETURNS ARE AT A 10+ YEAR LOW
articles.summary: We can’t say we’re surprised. Down rounds, inertia in the IPO market, and climbing rates of administrations were always going to impact VC returns. The second order impact of this is that LPs are likely going to be a little hesitant giving GPs fresh capital, which impacts the amount of funding available for startups.

articles.title: M&A MAKES A STRONG COMEBACK IN THE DACH REGION
articles.summary: The absence of growth funding in the DACH region has been replaced with healthy M&A. The number of M&A transactions in the region has increased from 153 in H1 2022 to 235 in H1 2023, a 54% increase. Last month LeanIX was acquired for a reported €1.2bn by software giant SAP in one of Germany’s biggest ever tech acquisitions.

articles.title: SELLING IN STEALTH, CATEGORY CREATION AND EXPANSION
articles.summary: Ryan Lieber was the 40th hire at Snowflake back in 2014 when it was still in stealth. Starting as an SDR, he’s since worked on country expansion and program management. This piece explores the challenges of building a category-creating business and a few lessons learned selling along the way: the optimal SDR:AE ratio, pipeline health checks, and involving engineers in sales calls.

articles.title: TIPS FOR GATHERING PRODUCT FEEDBACK
articles.summary: Customers don’t really know what they want. Very few of them are able to envision and articulate new product ideas. Instead of asking them what they want, ask them more about their problems. The solution will start to become more apparent.

articles.title: 11 YEARS OF SAAS PRODUCT STRATEGY
articles.summary: Reflections on Tanda’s transition into a multi-product company, with key takeaways including the value of diversifying products, adjusting ineffective features, and prioritizing customer-driven demand.

articles.title: GOING FROM DEVELOPER TO CEO
articles.summary: You’re either a builder or a seller right? This is a rundown from an engineer who turned into a founder and then switched to the business side of the operation. The biggest takeaway is how the move meant they ended up juggling lots of work streams and had to get good at fast context switching.

articles.title: THE TOP METRICS FOR USAGE-BASED SAAS
articles.summary: Usage-based billing seems to be growing in popularity. How do you know if it’s working? Here are the 14 top metrics you should be keeping an eye on.

articles.title: WHO CAN COMMERCIALIZE NEW TECHNOLOGIES?
articles.summary: Investors often prioritize easily identifiable metrics, such as founder pedigree, especially when dealing with novel technologies that are still emerging. However, as technologies mature, the focus on founder backgrounds might not necessarily yield the best innovative ventures. Instead, the fusion of top-tier research with creative product perspectives might be the key to pushing forward in industries like AI, biotech, robotics, and more.

articles.title: ON GETTING IT RIGHT THE FIRST TIME
articles.summary: As founders, we’re sometimes perfectionists. Get comfortable with the fact that the first version is never the final version. Doing something great is a series of iterations.

articles.title: SELLING INTO THE FUTURE
articles.summary: To win a government contract, Fairchild Semiconductor quoted products significantly below market rate. It did this because it was confident that over time its manufacturing costs would come down significantly. Is there something we can learn from this and apply to selling AI products? Compute costs will no doubt come down, chips will get better, and hardware will improve.
